title: Fort Anjediva
tagline: Fort Anjediva — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Fort Anjediva, built on the Anjadip Island, off the coast of the Indian state of Karnataka but under the administrative jurisdiction of the Indian state of Goa, was once under Port
overview.description: Fort Anjediva, built on the Anjadip Island, off the coast of the Indian state of Karnataka but under the administrative jurisdiction of the Indian state of Goa, was once under Portuguese rule. The island of Anjadip has an area of 1.5 square kilometres (0.58 mi2). Though the fort has a rich history linked to rule in Portuguese India, the fort is presently in ruins. The historic church on the island in its vicinity, the Church of Our Lady of Springs, was built in 1505.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Karwar
topPlaces.description: Karwar is a coastal city and the administrative headquarters of Uttara Kannada district, formerly part of the Bombay Presidency, located at the mouth of the Kali river along the Konkan Coast in the present-day state of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: INS Kadamba
topPlaces.description: INS Kadamba or Naval Base Karwar is an Indian Navy base located near Karwar in Karnataka. The base, which was commissioned on 31 May 2005, is being developed under the codename, Project Seabird.

topPlaces.name: Church of Our Lady of Springs
topPlaces.description: The Church of Our Lady of Springs (Portuguese: Igreja de Nossa Senhora das Brotas) is located in the vicinity of the Fort Anjediva on the Anjadip Island, off the west Coast of India under the administrative control of Goa, India. Initially, in 1502, it was built as a small thatched chapel, but later, in 1506, was made into a more permanent structure.

image.alt: Church of Our Lady of Springs

topPlaces.name: Anjediva Island
topPlaces.description: Anjediva Island (also Anjadip Island) (Goan Konkani: Anjadiv; Portuguese: Ilha de Angediva) is an island in the Arabian Sea. It sits off the coast of Karwar, Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: INS Chapal
topPlaces.description: INS Chapal (K94) was a Chamak class missile boat of the Indian Navy. It is now a museum ship on Rabindranath Tagore Beach in Karwar, Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: INHS Patanjali
topPlaces.description: INHS Patanjali is the naval hospital of the Indian Navy at Karwar, Karnataka which was commissioned on 26 December 2006. INHS Patanjali is the second naval establishment commissioned at Karwar.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya Karwar
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya Karwar is a central government school in Karwar, Karnataka State, India. The school was inaugurated in 2005.

topPlaces.name: Nandangadda
topPlaces.description: Nandangadda is a coastal village located in Karwar Taluka of Uttara Kannada district, in the Indian state of Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fort Anjediva?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fort Anjediva is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fort Anjediva?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (57 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fort Anjediva?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fort Anjediva at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fort Anjediva?
faq.a: Stays in Fort Anjediva start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fort Anjediva?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karwar, INS Kadamba, Church of Our Lady of Springs, Anjediva Island, INS Chapal.
